_readme: Render recipe. This SHO target renders via render_engine=nightlight: the reference author's own open tool, whose STAR-NEUTRAL colour balance recovers the O3 sphere that our in-house chain's SPCC photometric fit equalises away (NOTES dead ends). The `nightlight` block tunes the develop; starcomb detects the mono-filters narrowband composition and delegates.

approved.look: honest green developed SHO — the filled O3 sphere + dust recovered via the star-neutral balance + one global stretch, no star-sep/corings/denoise. The shell is genuinely Ha-dominant (measured O3/Ha 0.45), so green is the data's true colour; the reference's teal is an explicit O3-emphasis (nightlight.o3_emphasis) plus the author's manual GIMP finish.

notes.render: render_engine=nightlight — SPCC is NOT the render colour step here (its photometric fit equalises O3=Ha and erases the sphere). The spcc block stays for the compose/solve pipeline and the reference _spcc stack.
notes.calib: prebuilt-master ingest: the corpus ships header-bare masters (calib/{dark,flat}_<token>.fits); per-filter darks pair with the per-filter exposures (Ha 600 s / S2 500 s / O3 400 s)
notes.sphere_RESOLVED: the O3 sphere + dust are recovered by the Nightlight path: neutralising the mean star colour (stars carry ~no O3) boosts O3 several-fold and reveals the sphere; one global stretch develops the dust; no corings/denoise. A prior CRESCENT was our per-channel member stacks being mutually MISALIGNED (O3 offset ~57 px) fed raw — nightlight_sho aligns them and reports the offset.
